summary: Iteration 4 frontend testing for jumpable platforms, obstacles, power-ups, throw-book mechanic. Found and fixed a CRITICAL syntax error at end of /app/frontend/app/game.tsx (3 lines of duplicate/garbage StyleSheet entries after the closing `});` block) which would otherwise break the bundle. After fix, app loads cleanly: Title -> Start -> Maya (select-female-librarian-button) -> confirm-character-button -> game-screen renders with no runtime errors. HUD now shows all 3 buttons in correct order (throw-book-button '📚 0' opacity 0.4, mute-toggle-button '🔊', pause-game-button '‖'). Tap on throw with 0 ammo correctly does nothing. Pause overlay opens during play and resume works. Mute toggle icon switches 🔊→🔇. Reached LEVEL 2 in extended play confirming level transitions and obstacle-spawn gating (L2+) is wired. Code review confirms all required hooks per request: Entity union has platform/obstacle/powerup/playerBook; entityDims covers all kinds; physics has platform-landing pre-pass before ground clamp (vy>=0 descent check, prevBottom <= e.y+4); collision dispatcher handles platform (skip), obstacle/sign-style damage and powerup branches via spawn loop; playerBook movement loop checks banner+boss collisions (+30 BOOK / -1 HP); throwBook decrements ammoRef and pushes playerBook entity; restart() resets ammoRef=0 and setAmmo(0). Power-up actual pickup is probabilistic (~5% spawn) and was not observed during the live ~60s play session due to randomness + frequent jumps causing player to clear power-up vertical band — but underlying code path is verified correct. No runtime/page errors captured.

issues: Repeated console warnings: 'textShadow*' style props are deprecated. Use 'textShadow'.
Repeated console warnings: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Cosmetic only — does not break gameplay.

action_items: Optional: address React Native deprecation warnings (textShadow*, pointerEvents prop) in game.tsx & overlays.

Optional: consider raising power-up spawn rate slightly (e.g. 8-10%) or lowering spawn Y so it's reachable when player runs on ground without jumping — current spawn at GROUND_Y - POWERUP_SIZE - 70 yields only ~8px Y overlap with grounded player, making collection rare in non-active play. Functionally correct but UX-rare.

Main agent self-test recommended: open /game directly, run for 60-90s with active jumping/idling and confirm at least one '+3 BOOKS!' float appears, throw button label increments to '📚 3', and a thrown book sprite-book-like rectangle launches rightward and one-shots a banner enemy for +30.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: /app/frontend/app/game.tsx ended with duplicate orphan lines after the styles closing `});` (lines 1105-1107 garbage). This is a hard syntax error that would crash Metro bundling. Removed.
Entity & physics implementation matches the spec items (a)-(g) listed in the request.
Throw button correctly shows opacity 0.4 + muted gold border when ammoRef=0 — accessible visual state.
PlayerBook collision check iterates entities.current within the outer for-loop, which is acceptable here (small N) but is O(n^2) per frame — fine for current scale.
updated_files: /app/frontend/app/game.tsx (removed 3 trailing garbage lines causing TS/JS syntax error)

success_rate.frontend: 8/9 verifiable items passed; powerup-pickup observation not captured due to randomness

context_for_next_testing_agent: On title screen the start button testID is 'start-game-button' (not 'start-button'). Female character is 'select-female-librarian-button' followed by 'confirm-character-button'. The game-screen testIDs include: throw-book-button, mute-toggle-button, pause-game-button, touch-zone-jump, score-value, lives-row, player-sprite, level-intro, pause-overlay, game-over-overlay. Power-up entities don't carry their own testID — they are visual-only Views. To deterministically verify ammo gain in automation, consider injecting a power-up via a debug hook or temporarily raising the spawn probability.
rca_of_issue: Root cause of the original blocker: a partial copy/paste left 3 stray lines after StyleSheet.create({...}) closing brace in game.tsx. Reproduction: open the app, white screen with metro bundling error. Mitigation: removed the garbage lines so file ends cleanly with `});` after styles.
